On this page

Settings ​

All settings are accessible from Media → AI Image Renamer in the WordPress admin.

API Configuration ​

Enable Auto-Rename ​

Toggle the plugin on or off. When disabled, images are uploaded with their original filenames.

  • Default: Off
  • Option key: enabled

Groq API Key ​

Your Groq API key is required for the plugin to function. Keys start with gsk_ and are encrypted before being stored in the database using defuse/php-encryption.

  • Option key: api_key

Secure your encryption key

By default, the encryption key is stored in the WordPress database. For better security, define it in your wp-config.php:

php
define('AIR_ENCRYPTION_KEY', 'your-long-random-key-here');

You can generate a key using the defuse/php-encryption library. See the Encryption Service documentation for details.

AI Model ​

Choose between two Llama 4 vision models:

ModelBest ForSpeedParameters
MaverickDetailed, creative analysis~600 tokens/s17B (128 Experts)
ScoutFast processing~750 tokens/s17B (16 Experts)
  • Default: Maverick
  • Option key: model

Use as Alt Text ​

When enabled, the AI-generated description is also saved as the image's alt attribute. This is great for accessibility and SEO.

When this option is active, the keyword limit is automatically set to 10 (regardless of the Max Keywords setting) to produce richer alt text.

  • Default: Off
  • Option key: set_alt_text

File Types ​

Select which image formats should be processed by the plugin.

FormatMIME TypeDefault
JPEGimage/jpeg✅ Enabled
PNGimage/png✅ Enabled
WebPimage/webp✅ Enabled
AVIFimage/avif✅ Enabled
GIFimage/gif❌ Disabled
  • Option key: file_types

Advanced Settings ​

Max Keywords ​

Controls how many keywords the AI generates for the filename. More keywords produce longer, more descriptive filenames.

  • Range: 1–10
  • Default: 5
  • Option key: max_keywords

TIP

A value of 3–5 works well for most use cases. Use higher values if you want very descriptive filenames.

Options Storage ​

All settings are stored as a single serialized array in the WordPress options table under the key air_options.

php
// Retrieve all settings programmatically
$options = get_option('air_options', []);

// Check if auto-rename is enabled
$enabled = $options['enabled'] ?? false;

// Get the current model
$model = $options['model'] ?? 'meta-llama/llama-4-maverick-17b-128e-instruct';
